---
title: "Teaching"
---

::: {.page-lede}
Courses in experimental methods, data science, and AI for the social sciences, with previous teaching at Lincoln, Brown, and King's College London.
:::

::: {.legend}

[[]{.level-dot .undergrad} Undergraduate]{.legend-item}
[[]{.level-dot .grad} Graduate]{.legend-item}
[[]{.level-dot .both} Both levels]{.legend-item}

:::

## Current courses

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[DATASCI 385 -- Experimental Methods](https://danilofreire.github.io/datasci385/) [Emory]{.institution-badge .ib-emory}

:::

[2024 to present. How to design and analyse experiments in the social sciences. We cover randomisation, power analysis, and causal inference, with worked examples in R.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[DATASCI 350 -- Data Science Computing](https://danilofreire.github.io/datasci350/) [Emory]{.institution-badge .ib-emory}

:::

[2024 to present. Computational tools for data science. Topics include version control, the command line, cloud computing, and reproducible workflows.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[DATASCI 185 -- Introduction to AI Applications](https://danilofreire.github.io/datasci185/) [Emory]{.institution-badge .ib-emory}

:::

[2025 to present. An introduction to AI for undergraduates. We look at how large language models work, how to write effective prompts, and how to think about ethics, bias, policy, and governance.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[DATASCI 151 -- Introduction to Statistical Computing II](https://danilofreire.github.io/datasci151/) [Emory]{.institution-badge .ib-emory}

:::

[2024 to present. Python programming for data analysis. The course walks through data wrangling, visualisation, and basic statistical modelling.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

## Workshops

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[EGAP Learning Days 15: Field Experiments in the Social Sciences](https://egap.org/project/learning-days-15-latin-america-regional-hub-workshop/) [EGAP]{.institution-badge .ib-other}

:::

[Medellín, August 2024. Taught with Rosario Aguilar, Jake Bowers, Luis Maldonado, and Julia Seither.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[EGAP Learning Days 13: Field Experiments in the Social Sciences](https://egap.org/project/learning-days-13-latin-america-regional-hub-workshop/) [EGAP]{.institution-badge .ib-other}

:::

[Mexico City, August 2023. Taught with Rosario Aguilar, Jake Bowers, Luis Maldonado, and Maarten Voors.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

## Previous courses

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Policing Crime and Deviance](https://blackboard.lincoln.ac.uk/ultra/courses/_182116_1/cl/outline) [Lincoln]{.institution-badge .ib-lincoln}

:::

[2021 to 2024. Theories of policing, criminal behaviour, and deviance, with attention to accountability, legitimacy, and comparative systems. Co-taught with Anthony Ellis and Elena Gaschino.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Human Rights](https://blackboard.lincoln.ac.uk/ultra/courses/_184943_1/cl/outline) [Lincoln]{.institution-badge .ib-lincoln}

:::

[2021 to 2024. Foundations, institutions, and contemporary debates in human rights, drawing on political theory, international law, and empirical case studies. Co-taught with Nick Cowen and Aris Trantidis.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[POLS 1824W -- Political Violence](https://danilofreire.github.io/pols1824w/) [Brown]{.institution-badge .ib-brown}

:::

[Fall 2019. Causes and consequences of political violence. Topics include civil wars, terrorism, state repression, and organised crime.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[POLS 1600 -- Political Research Methods](https://pols1600.github.io/) [Brown]{.institution-badge .ib-brown}

:::

[Winter 2019. Research design and quantitative methods for political science. The course covers measurement, inference, and data analysis in R.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .grad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Political Economy and Institutions](https://github.com/danilofreire/economia-politica-instituicoes-ufm) [OMMA / UFM]{.institution-badge .ib-other}

:::

[2017. Graduate seminar on institutional economics, property rights, and the political foundations of economic development.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .undergrad}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Principles of Economics (I & II)](https://github.com/danilofreire/core-econ) [King's College London]{.institution-badge .ib-kcl}

:::

[2017. Teaching assistant for microeconomics and macroeconomics, covering markets, firm behaviour, and monetary and fiscal policy. With David Skarbek and Konstantinos Matakos.]{.teach-desc}

:::
::::

## Suggested courses

Syllabi available for download:

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Machine Learning for Political Science](syllabi/freire-ml-polsci.pdf)

:::
:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Latin American Politics](syllabi/freire-latam-politics.pdf)

:::
:::
::::

:::: {.teach-entry}

[]{.level-dot .both}

::: {.teach-body}
::: {.teach-title}

[Introduction to Comparative Politics](syllabi/freire-comparative-politics.pdf)

:::
:::
::::
